<content>1: # Task
2: 
3: ## Issue Title
4: 
5: Improve Login Page UI Design Consistency
6: ### Description
7: Update the visual design and layout of the login page to match the design specification below and align with the existing design system standards.
8: 
9: ### Design Specification
10: 
11: > **Note**: The original Figma reference at `https://sample-banana-62009166.figma.site` requires JavaScript to render. Below is a complete text description of the design extracted from that reference.
12: 
13: #### Page Layout
14: - **Full-page background**: Light gray/off-white (`bg-muted` or `bg-secondary`)
15: - **Content is vertically and horizontally centered** on the page
16: - **RTL layout** (Hebrew text, right-to-left direction)
17: 
18: #### Header Section (above the card)
19: - **Headline**: "שלום, מוכנים להצליח?" — large text (~text-4xl), bold, dark color, centered
20: - **Subtitle**: "A-Guy המורה הפרטי שלכם" — smaller text (~text-base), muted/gray color, centered
21: - Generous vertical spacing between header and card (~py-16 or similar)
22: 
23: #### Login Card
24: - **White background** with rounded corners (`rounded-xl` or similar)
25: - **Subtle shadow** (box-shadow, e.g. `shadow-lg`)
26: - **Centered**, max-width roughly `max-w-md` (wider than current `max-w-sm`)
27: - **Generous internal padding** (~p-8 or p-10)
28: 
29: #### Card Contents (top to bottom)
30: 1. **Small decorative horizontal line** centered at top of card (a short `border-t` or `<hr>` element, ~w-12, accent color)
31: 2. **Section label**: "כניסה מהירה" (Quick Entry) — centered, medium text, dark color
32: 3. **Google Sign-In button**: Full-width, white/light background, bordered (`border`), rounded, with Google "G" icon on the right side and text "המשך עם Google" (Continue with Google). Prominent, large button.
33: 4. **Divider section**: Visual separator between Google button and signup link
34: 5. **Signup CTA button**: "הרשמה ללא עלות" (Free Registration) — styled as a secondary/outline button or accent link with background. Centered.
35: 6. **Footer text**: "גישה מהירה ומאובטחת." (Fast and secure access) and "בלחיצה אחת אתם בפנים." (One click and you're in) — small text, muted color, centered
36: 
37: #### Page Footer
38: - **Help link**: "?זקוקים לעזרה" (Need help?) with a help/question-mark icon, centered at very bottom of page, small muted text
39: 
40: #### Key Differences from Current Implementation
41: 1. **Background**: Current has no page background; design has light gray bg
42: 2. **Card width**: Current is `max-w-sm`; design appears wider (~`max-w-md`)
43: 3. **Card shadow**: Current has default card shadow; design has more prominent shadow
44: 4. **Decorative line**: Design has a small centered line above the "כניסה מהירה" text
45: 5. **No email/password fields visible**: The design shows Google-only login (no email/password form)
46: 6. **Signup CTA**: Design has a prominent signup button that current implementation lacks in this style
47: 7. **Footer help text**: Design has descriptive text and a help link not present in current
48: 8. **Card padding**: Design appears to have more generous internal spacing
49: 
50: ### Acceptance Criteria
51: - [ ] The login page layout matches the design specification above
52: - [ ] Full-page light background applied (`bg-muted` or similar design token)
53: - [ ] Card is wider (`max-w-md`), has prominent shadow, and generous padding
54: - [ ] Small decorative line appears above "כניסה מהירה" section label
55: - [ ] Google Sign-In button is full-width with Google icon
56: - [ ] Signup CTA button appears below Google button
57: - [ ] Footer text with help link appears at bottom of page
58: - [ ] All UI elements utilize established design system tokens and components
59: - [ ] Interactive states (hover, focus, disabled) use design system tokens
60: - [ ] Layout adapts gracefully across responsive breakpoints
61: - [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors, lint and format pass
62: 
63: ### Context
64: - **Design Reference**: Originally at `https://sample-banana-62009166.figma.site` (requires JavaScript, not fetchable by CI bots)
65: - **Files to modify**: `src/app/(frontend)/login/LoginPageContent.tsx`, `src/app/(frontend)/login/LoginForm.tsx`
66: - **Design system**: See `DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` and `tailwind.config.mjs` for available tokens
67: - **UI components**: Available in `src/ui/web/components/` (button, card, input, label, etc.)

(End of file - total 67 lines)
</content>

23:   messages: Messages

9: ## Summary
10: 
11: The `guest-session.ts` service uses `'guest-sessions' as const` type casts on 7 Payload API calls (lines 150, 178, 201, 222, 239, 265, 286). These are unnecessary because the `'guest-sessions'` collection is properly registered in Payload's type system (`payload-types.ts` line 77). The test factory `guest-session.factory.ts` also uses `as any` on line 73 and `as any` on line 109. All these should be removed for proper type safety.
12: 
13: Additionally, the service defines a manual `GuestSessionDoc` interface (lines 23-35) that duplicates the auto-generated `GuestSession` type from `payload-types.ts`. The manual interface should be replaced with the generated one to prevent type drift. There are also multiple `as unknown as GuestSessionDoc` and `as GuestSessionDoc` casts that become unnecessary once we use the proper generated type.
14: 
15: ## Assumptions
16: 
17: - The `GuestSession` type from `payload-types.ts` is the source of truth and matches the collection schema
18: - Removing `as const` will still compile because `'guest-sessions'` is a known literal in the `Config` type
19: - The `as any` casts in the factory are workarounds for the same perceived type issue

# Plan: Fix guest-sessions collection type safety

**Task ID**: 260225-auto-90
**Task Type**: fix_bug
**Branch**: fix/260225-auto-90-guest-sessions-type-safety

---

## Summary

The `guest-session.ts` service uses `'guest-sessions' as const` type casts on 7 Payload API calls (lines 150, 178, 201, 222, 239, 265, 286). These are unnecessary because the `'guest-sessions'` collection is properly registered in Payload's type system (`payload-types.ts` line 77). The test factory `guest-session.factory.ts` also uses `as any` on line 73 and `as any` on line 109. All these should be removed for proper type safety.

Additionally, the service defines a manual `GuestSessionDoc` interface (lines 23-35) that duplicates the auto-generated `GuestSession` type from `payload-types.ts`. The manual interface should be replaced with the generated one to prevent type drift. There are also multiple `as unknown as GuestSessionDoc` and `as GuestSessionDoc` casts that become unnecessary once we use the proper generated type.

## Assumptions

- The `GuestSession` type from `payload-types.ts` is the source of truth and matches the collection schema
- Removing `as const` will still compile because `'guest-sessions'` is a known literal in the `Config` type
- The `as any` casts in the factory are workarounds for the same perceived type issue
- Existing unit tests mock Payload, so they will still pass after type changes
